_metadata.description: State insurance requirements for contractors — update when state laws change
_metadata.source: State licensing board regulations and state labor department requirements

CA.workers_comp: Required for all employers (no minimum employees)
CA.general_liability: Not required by state, but strongly recommended
CA.bond: $25,000 contractor license bond required (increasing to $25,000 in 2026)
CA.notes: Workers comp exempt if sole owner with no employees — must file exemption certificate

TX.workers_comp: Not required by state, but recommended. Required for government contracts.
TX.notes: Texas is one of few states where workers comp is optional for private employers

FL.general_liability: Not required by state, but often required by clients
FL.bond: Varies; some local jurisdictions require performance bonds
FL.notes: Construction industry has stricter workers comp rules than other industries

NY.general_liability: Required for NYC home improvement contractors
NY.bond: NYC requires $5,000 bond for home improvement contractors
NY.notes: NYC licensing is separate from state; requires both workers comp and disability insurance
